summary: Hydration shows an over-broad Control UI stale-selection cluster split across agent-switch drift, explicit reload drift, and initial-load select-state bugs. Kept #39392 as the live canonical issue for the agent-switch subfamily, left all open PRs non-mutating, and escalated only the unresolved maintainer choices around the surviving agent-switch PR path and the broad reload PR #40443.

actions.reason: Best live canonical issue for the agent-switch stale primary-model selector family.
Directly overlapped by the stronger same-scope PR #39495; kept non-mutating until maintainers choose the surviving PR.
Useful narrow candidate for #39392, but not the settled canonical PR path from the available artifact.
Reload-specific report is tentatively covered by #40443, but it should stay open until that broader fix path is either accepted or narrowed.
Naive reload fix is superseded by the safer explicit-reload path in #40443.
Maintainer judgment is required before using #40443 as the reload canonical path or superseding other reload PRs with it.
Broader regression bundle with unique save-path behavior; not safely deduped into the narrower switch/reload UI cluster.
Initial-load option-selection approach is functionally superseded by the later follow-up PR #52948.
Useful candidate, but not the safest live canonical PR path from the available artifact.
Keep open as related work; it is not a safe cluster-wide canonical replacement for the live switch or reload issues.
Related follow-up, but too broad and under-hydrated to use as the canonical path from this run.
Maintainer must choose whether #70633 becomes the surviving PR for the agent-switch/first-load subfamily before any supersede or merge recommendation.

needs_human: Choose the surviving PR for the agent-switch stale-selector family: #39495 is the clean keyed-remount fix, #70633 is the newer select-binding fix, and #46275/#54724 are broader variants; the artifact does not prove which one should supersede the others.
Decide whether #40443 is acceptable as the reload-specific fix for #40352 or whether a narrower follow-up is required; the PR is broad and still has hydrated Codex review concerns.
